a_heading: Young People of the World — This Is Your Moment
a_quote_tbd: Placeholder — awaiting the final Call to Action text
a_quote: History is not something that happens to you — it is something you make. We call on the youth of every continent to lift their eyes above the crises of the day, and to join us in building a world of sovereign nations, shared development, and the unstoppable power of human creativity.
a_stat1_s: where the youth movement is organizing today
a_stat2_s: united by the power of ideas, not divided by geopolitics
b_heading: Viewing Africa’s Current Crisis from the Vantage Point of Universal History
b_lede_html: Excerpted from the speech delivered by Lyndon H. LaRouche, Jr. on April 26, 1997, to the Schiller Institute conference “Peace Through Development in Africa’s Great Lakes Region,” Walluf, Germany. First published in EIR, Vol. 24, No. 22, May 23, 1997.
b_fig1_alt: Lyndon LaRouche addressing a room of African diplomats and guests at the Schiller Institute conference in Walluf, Germany, 1997
b_fig1_cap_html: Lyndon LaRouche addresses African diplomats and guests at the Schiller Institute conference “Peace Through Development in Africa’s Great Lakes Region,” Walluf, Germany, April 26, 1997. EIRNS/Christopher Lewis
b_p1_html: Although I am interested in many of these subjects, and involved in many of the topics which will be discussed today, I thought it more appropriate that I address the one topic which no one else would address, which is the situation in the world, which defines the situation in Africa.
b_p2: Now, I ask you to step out of being Africans for a moment, and go up to the top of a mountain, where you can see in the distance both the breadth of this planet’s population, and also, see into its past, several hundred years. And, look at the situation that we’re in; look down on this planet on which you happen to live, but stay on the mountaintop for a while, and say, What is going on, around the whole world?
b_p3_html: Let’s look at Africa, and its development, and its pain of today, in terms of what is happening around the world. And what you see is, this planetary civilization is disintegrating!
b_p4: We’re on the verge, in the process actually, of the greatest financial collapse, globally, in all human history. We’re at the point that the monetary systems of every country, with possibly the exception of China, can disintegrate on a given morning. That is, we could have a chain reaction in financial speculation, which, within 48 to 72 hours, can annihilate every currency, every bank on this planet, simply because everything freezes; money is no longer negotiable, because of the collapse. That can happen.
b_fig2_alt: Franklin D. Roosevelt reviewing troops in Liberia, 1943
b_fig2_cap_html: President Franklin Roosevelt in Liberia, 1943 — from the same EIR article.
b_fig3_alt: Godfrey Binaisa, former President of Uganda
b_fig3_cap_html: Godfrey Binaisa, former President of Uganda — from the same EIR article.
b_readmore_html: Continue reading — the full speech traces mankind’s nature as a creature of ideas, and Africa’s place in universal history.
c_heading: Ten Principles of a New International Security & Development Architecture
c_lede: Proposed by Schiller Institute founder Helga Zepp-LaRouche, November 30, 2022. Click each principle to read it.
p01_b: The new International Security and Development Architecture must be a partnership of perfectly sovereign nation-states, based on the Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence and the UN Charter.
p02_b: The absolute priority must be to alleviate poverty in every nation on the planet — easily possible, if the existing technologies are used for the benefit of the common good.
p03_t: Modern health systems for every country
p03_b: The life expectancy of all people living must be prolonged to the fullest potential by creating modern health systems in every country on the planet.
p04_t: Universal education for every child and adult
p04_b: One of the main aims of the new architecture must be providing access to universal education for every child and every adult — developing the creative potential of all.
p05_b: The international financial system must be reorganized, so that it can provide productive credits to accomplish these aims.
p06_t: A World Land-Bridge connecting all continents
p06_b_html: The new economic order must focus on creating the conditions for modern industries and agriculture, starting with the infrastructural development of all continents — eventually connected by tunnels and bridges to become a World Land-Bridge.
p07_t: End geopolitics — no division into blocs
p07_b: The new global security architecture must eliminate the concept of geopolitics by ending the division of the world into blocs.
p08_b: For the first time — because of nuclear weapons, pandemics, the internet, and other global effects — mankind is sitting in one boat. Our challenges can only be solved together.
p09_t: In cohesion with the physical universe
p09_b: The economic, social and political order has to be brought into cohesion with the lawfulness of the physical universe.
p10_b: Man is fundamentally good, and capable of infinitely perfecting the creativity of his mind and the beauty of his soul — the most advanced geological force in the universe.
d_lede_html: Conceived by Lyndon and Helga LaRouche in the early 1990s as a global policy of “Peace Through Development” — corridors of rail, water, and power uniting every continent. Click a pulsing marker to explore a featured project.
d_map_alt: Map of the World Land-Bridge showing proposed development corridors across all continents, including the Oasis Plan, Transaqua, the Bering Strait tunnel, China's BRI, NAWAPA, Chancay Port and the Bioceanic Corridor
d_map_hint: Click a marker to explore a project
d1_p_html: A fifty-year development perspective for Palestine and Israel, first proposed by Lyndon LaRouche in 1975: peace built not on paper treaties, but on the physical economy — water, power, and transport for all the peoples of the region.
d1_f1: Nuclear-powered desalination to create abundant fresh water where geopolitics now fights over scarcity
d1_f2: New canals, rail corridors, and development cities linking the region into the World Land-Bridge
d1_f3: Development as the true basis for a durable peace
d1_video_title: The Oasis Plan — Schiller Institute video
d1_video_aria: Play: The Oasis Plan. Loads the video from YouTube.
d2_p_html: The great project to refill Lake Chad — transferring a small fraction of the water of the Congo River basin northward through a ~2,400 km navigable canal, bringing water, power, and transport to the heart of Africa.
d2_f1: Would benefit some 40 million people across 12 nations
d2_f2: A navigable waterway spine for inland Africa: irrigation, hydropower, and commerce
d2_f3: Restores the shrinking Lake Chad — turning a security crisis into a development driver
d2_video_aria: Play: Transaqua (demo video). Loads the video from YouTube.
d3_draft_note: ⬡ Draft copy — the pamphlet document leaves this section open; the projects below are taken from the World Land-Bridge map and can be replaced.
d3_p_html: The deepest-water port in South America, at Chancay, Peru, joins a bioceanic rail corridor linking the Pacific coast to the Atlantic coast of Brazil — cutting Shanghai-to-South-America shipping times by up to a quarter and opening the continent’s interior to development.
d3_f1: 8,000+ direct jobs and an estimated $4.5 billion in annual revenue
d3_f2: Rail spine magnifying the impact of the port across the whole continent
d3_f3: South America joins the World Land-Bridge
d3_video_aria: Play: Chancay Port & Bioceanic Corridor (demo video). Loads the video from YouTube.
d4_sub: Featured project · The Bronx, New York
d4_draft_note: ⬡ Placeholder — the featured Bronx project is to be announced.
d4_p: The World Land-Bridge is not only about distant continents — it is a mission for every community. The Bronx section will feature the youth movement’s local organizing and a concrete development project for New York, connecting the neighborhood to the world.
d4_f1: Featured project text to be supplied
d4_f3: How to get involved on the ground

hc_title_html:
International Online Youth Conference
“Young People of the World, Unite!”
hc_p: Young people from across six continents gathered online to take up the fight for a new paradigm. Watch the highlights, then take your place in what comes next.
f1_p: Connect directly with youth-movement organizers worldwide. Scan the QR code or tap the button.
f2_p_html: Executive Intelligence Review — the weekly journal founded by Lyndon LaRouche. Get your free subscription and study the ideas behind the movement.
f3_p: Leave your email and a local organizer will reach out about joining the International Youth Movement.
f3_consent_text: I agree that my email is stored to contact me about the Youth Movement.

js_mideast_text: LaRouche's fifty-year development plan for Palestine and Israel: nuclear-powered desalination, new water, transport and power infrastructure — peace built on physical development.
js_africa_text: Water transfer from the Congo River basin to refill Lake Chad — a 2,400 km navigable canal benefiting some 40 million people across 12 nations, with hydropower, irrigation, and inland navigation.
js_samerica_text: The deepest-water port in South America joined to a Pacific–Atlantic rail corridor across Peru and Brazil — 8,000+ direct jobs and a gateway connecting the continent to the World Land-Bridge. (Draft selection — final featured project pending.)
js_bronx_text: The youth movement's local organizing hub — the featured Bronx development project will be announced. This marker links the neighborhood to the world.
